title: The One With The Proposal (2)

jokes.text: You say he's straight, I'll believe you.
jokes.setup: Richard is professing his love to Monica and mentions Chandler
jokes.punchline: Casually questions Chandler's sexuality as an aside in a romantic declaration
jokes.explanation: Perfectly timed throwaway line that adds levity to an intense romantic moment. The deadpan delivery makes it work.

dialogue.speaker: Richard
dialogue.line: Monica, I love you. You mean everything to me. And Chandler... well, is he even interested in women?

jokes.text: Why don't they put chairs back here?
jokes.setup: Richard is in the middle of a passionate romantic declaration
jokes.punchline: Suddenly gets distracted by the practical discomfort of standing
jokes.explanation: Good tension-breaker that feels natural and relatable. Undercuts the romance with mundane practicality.

dialogue.speaker: Richard
dialogue.line: Monica, I love you. I've always loved you. You mean everything to me, and I want to spend the rest of my life with—ow, my back. God, my back is killing me. Can we sit down?

jokes.text: What were you doing in Africa? -Working with blind kids.
jokes.setup: Richard mentions spending six months in Africa to forget Monica
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was doing impossibly noble charity work, making him even more appealing
jokes.explanation: Classic sitcom move - making an already perfect guy even more perfect. Monica's exasperated delivery sells it.

dialogue.speaker: Richard
dialogue.line: I spent six months in Africa trying to forget you.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, really?

dialogue.speaker: Richard
dialogue.line: Yes. I was building schools and digging wells for villages that had never had clean water.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's... that's actually really sweet.

dialogue.speaker: Richard
dialogue.line: I know. It made me realize what's important in life.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: So you got over me?

dialogue.speaker: Richard
dialogue.line: Not even a little bit.

jokes.text: What are you doing to me?
jokes.setup: Learning Richard was helping blind kids in Africa
jokes.punchline: Monica's frustrated recognition that he's becoming irresistible
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Monica's internal conflict, but fairly standard romantic comedy beat.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: He's in Africa helping blind kids? Oh come on! That's not fair! He's already impossibly handsome, he's successful, he's sophisticated... and now he's out there saving the world? He's becoming completely irresistible!

jokes.text: Because of the government.
jokes.setup: Richard asks why Monica is making Chandler wait for marriage
jokes.punchline: Monica gives a completely nonsensical answer about the government
jokes.explanation: Perfect non-sequitur that shows Monica scrambling for an excuse. The randomness works because it's so obviously deflection.

dialogue.speaker: Richard
dialogue.line: Why are you making Chandler wait for marriage?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, if we get married before the government says it's okay, they can take away our tax exemptions and give them to the Presbyterians!

jokes.text: I'm so happy and not at all jealous. Oh, no! God, definitely not jealous.
jokes.setup: Rachel claims to be happy about Monica and Chandler's engagement
jokes.punchline: Immediately and transparently contradicts herself with obvious jealousy
jokes.explanation: Standard jealousy denial bit, but Jennifer Aniston's delivery would sell the transparent obviousness of it.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, I'm so happy for you guys!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I mean, it's not fair. Why does Monica get to be happy? I'm supposed to be the pretty one with the good life.

jokes.text: I'm probably 98% happy and maybe 2% jealous.
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to quantify her feelings about the engagement
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific percentages that highlight her overthinking
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor showing Rachel's analytical nature, but not particularly original.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I'm 87% sure I want to get married. 9% terrified. 3% wondering if I left the stove on. And 1% thinking about that guy from the coffee place.

jokes.text: What's 2%? That's nothing. Totally.
jokes.setup: Rachel's 2% jealousy comment
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's overly supportive dismissal of what's clearly significant jealousy
jokes.explanation: Phoebe being supportive while missing the obvious jealousy. Functional but not standout.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, 2%? That's nothing! I mean, you're barely jealous at all. You're like, the least jealous person I know. It's actually kind of inspiring how you're just totally fine with everything.

jokes.text: I'm like 90-10. Yeah. Me too.
jokes.setup: The percentage game continues
jokes.punchline: Both admit to much higher jealousy percentages, showing their earlier claims were lies
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that pays off the setup while revealing their true feelings. Solid structure.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Okay, fine. I was like 80% jealous.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I was 90% jealous!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I wanted to claw your face off!

jokes.text: No. But you know who was looking for you? Tenille.
jokes.setup: Joey asks if they've seen Chandler
jokes.punchline: Rachel deflects with a random name instead of being helpful
jokes.explanation: Weak deflection joke that doesn't really land. Random name-dropping without much payoff.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Have you guys seen Chandler?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh, um... Marcus? Have you seen Marcus?

jokes.text: Look at pigs. Let's take a second here and look at pigs.
jokes.setup: Joey explaining why marriage is unnatural
jokes.punchline: Seriously proposing pigs as a relationship model with mock-academic tone
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic - using animal behavior to justify his stance. The formal tone makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Look, marriage is unnatural. Humans aren't meant to be with one person forever.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You know who has it figured out? Pigs. Pigs don't get married. Pigs just... do their thing.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: We should be more like pigs. It's science.

jokes.text: A pig can have 100 sexual partners in a lifetime. And that's just an ordinary pig, not even a pig that's good at sports.
jokes.setup: Joey's pig relationship philosophy
jokes.punchline: Implies athletic ability would increase a pig's sexual success
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that takes absurd logic even further. The sports angle is unexpected and perfectly Joey. Highly quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: If a pig were athletic, he'd get more action.

jokes.text: Why don't you put down your copy of The Rules, huh, man-trap?
jokes.setup: Phoebe pressuring Joey about marriage
jokes.punchline: Joey accuses Phoebe of using dating manipulation tactics
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference joke that relies on audience familiarity with 'The Rules' book. Somewhat dated now.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You need to get married, Joey. You're not getting any younger.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa. Are you trying to manipulate me? Because that's what my ex-girlfriends do!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I'm not manipulating you, I'm just saying—

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No, no, I know manipulation when I see it. The guilt trips, the pressure about my age... that's textbook stuff!

jokes.text: That's what makes it so easy to be 80% happy for Monica and Chandler.
jokes.setup: Continuing the percentage theme from earlier
jokes.punchline: Rachel's jealousy percentage has increased even more
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Rachel's jealousy is growing. Nice structural payoff to earlier setup.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: My jealousy percentage has increased even more.

jokes.text: I locked him in years ago.
jokes.setup: Phoebe revealing she has Joey as a backup marriage partner
jokes.punchline: Describes securing Joey like a business deal or contract
jokes.explanation: Good use of business terminology for romantic arrangement. Shows Phoebe's practical approach to relationships.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I have Joey as my backup. You know, in case I get to like 40 and I'm not married, we're getting married.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yeah, we made a pact. It's all very official. I have it notarized.

jokes.text: But believe me, that is not how he wanted to seal the deal.
jokes.setup: Phoebe and Joey shook hands on their backup marriage deal
jokes.punchline: Implies Joey wanted to seal it sexually
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey characterization through Phoebe's story. The innuendo is implied but clear.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: So we're really doing this? We're gonna get married if we're both still single at 40?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, we shook on it. It's official.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, I think we should seal it with a kiss.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: A kiss? I was thinking something a little more... binding.

jokes.text: Have you seen Rachel? Or a mirror?
jokes.setup: Joey asks Monica if she's seen Rachel
jokes.punchline: Adds 'or a mirror' revealing his narcissism
jokes.explanation: Excellent Joey line that perfectly captures his vanity. Great economy - maximum character revelation in minimal words.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Monica, have you seen Rachel?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No, I haven't. Why?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Or a mirror.

jokes.text: It's for my boat. Cool, huh?
jokes.setup: Joey holding what's apparently a boat mirror
jokes.punchline: Reveals the mirror is actually for a practical boat purpose, not vanity
jokes.explanation: Mild misdirection that subverts expectations about Joey's vanity, but the payoff isn't strong enough.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, check it out! I got this mirror for my boat.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Joey, you don't have a boat.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I know, but when I do, I'm gonna need this. It's for seeing behind the boat when you're backing up.

jokes.text: What's the matter? Talk to the captain.
jokes.setup: Monica seems upset
jokes.punchline: Joey refers to himself as captain because of his boat
jokes.explanation: Shows Joey's pride in his boat ownership. Character-consistent but not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey Monica, what's wrong? You look upset.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'm fine, Joey.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No you're not. But don't worry, Captain Joey is here to help.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Captain? Since when are you a captain?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Since I got my boat! I'm Captain Joey now.

jokes.text: Chandler is a complex fellow. One who is unlikely to take a wife.
jokes.setup: Monica asking about Chandler's marriage views
jokes.punchline: Joey uses formal, archaic language to describe Chandler
jokes.explanation: Joey speaking like a Victorian gentleman is funny character comedy. The formal tone contrasts with his usual speech.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: The man doth protest too much, methinks. He speaketh of marriage as one speaketh of root canal.

jokes.text: Chandler loves marriage!
jokes.setup: Joey just told Monica that Chandler hates marriage
jokes.punchline: Immediately reverses his position when he realizes he's sabotaging them
jokes.explanation: Shows Joey realizing his mistake and trying to backtrack. Standard sitcom reversal.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Chandler hates marriage! He's not gonna want to get married!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What? He proposed to me!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh... oh no. Oh no no no. I didn't know that part. Forget everything I said. Chandler loves marriage! He's crazy about marriage!

jokes.text: You got that from what I said?
jokes.setup: Monica lists all the anti-marriage things Joey just said about Chandler
jokes.punchline: Joey acts surprised that his clear statements conveyed those meanings
jokes.explanation: Good Joey moment showing his obliviousness to his own words' impact. Nice callback structure.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You said marriage is a death trap, it's the end of freedom and adventure, and by the way, you're not even sure he likes women!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: So that's not exactly a ringing endorsement!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What? I was just making conversation!

jokes.text: No, like, 'What's that?'
jokes.setup: Ross saying there would be no surprises if he and Rachel got married since they've slept together
jokes.punchline: Implies Rachel had a surprised reaction to seeing his anatomy
jokes.explanation: Self-deprecating humor about sexual inadequacy. The line works through implication and awkwardness.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, there would be no surprises if we got married. I mean, we've already slept together.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Yeah, that's not really a surprise anymore.

jokes.text: Although, believe me, she wanted to do a lot more than that.
jokes.setup: Ross explaining he and Phoebe shook hands on their backup marriage deal
jokes.punchline: Implies Phoebe wanted sexual contact instead of handshake
jokes.explanation: Parallel structure to Phoebe's Joey comment, but Ross version feels less natural to his character.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: We shook hands on it.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, I thought you meant something else.

jokes.text: I was making a coconut phone with the Professor.
jokes.setup: Joey asking where Chandler has been
jokes.punchline: References Gilligan's Island while describing ridiculous activity
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference that may not land for all audiences. Relies on familiarity with classic TV show.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Where have you been?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I was on Gilligan's Island.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, I got stuck there. The Professor couldn't fix the boat.

jokes.text: And I would have if these damn boat shoes wouldn't keep flying off!
jokes.setup: Chandler explaining he was trying to stop Richard from bothering Monica
jokes.punchline: Blames his failure on impractical footwear
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup with Chandler's typical neurotic excuse-making. Visual element adds to the humor.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I was trying to stop him, but these shoes have no traction!

jokes.text: Oh, my God! I know! They suck!
jokes.setup: Chandler dramatically reveals Richard wants to marry Monica
jokes.punchline: Joey completely misses the point and responds about the boat shoes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey obliviousness. Great misdirection where the audience expects shock about Richard but gets shoe commentary.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Richard wants to marry Monica!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, Richard has a boat? I love boat shoes!

jokes.text: Look, us getting our asses kicked is not a solution.
jokes.setup: Chandler wanting to fight Richard
jokes.punchline: Joey pragmatically points out they would lose the fight
jokes.explanation: Good realistic response that shows Joey's unexpected practical wisdom. Undercuts typical male bravado.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa! You can't fight him. The guy's like a hundred years old!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: So? He'd kick your ass!

jokes.text: That coconut phone might be good for the boat.
jokes.setup: Chandler mentioned making a coconut phone earlier
jokes.punchline: Joey finds practical boat application for the ridiculous prop
jokes.explanation: Minor callback that shows Joey's boat obsession. Functional but not particularly strong.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, wait a minute. If I tie two of these coconut phones together, I could make a boat!

jokes.text: I didn't ask.
jokes.setup: Monica says she doesn't know why she's at Richard's apartment
jokes.punchline: Richard points out he didn't actually ask for an explanation
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response that shows Richard's directness. Economic delivery makes it work.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I don't know why I'm at your apartment.

dialogue.speaker: Richard
dialogue.line: I didn't ask for an explanation.

jokes.text: I'll just leave the door open and go sit on the couch.
jokes.setup: Monica hesitating about coming into his apartment
jokes.punchline: Richard's overly careful approach to avoid any impropriety
jokes.explanation: Shows Richard being considerate about the awkward situation. Mild humor from over-politeness.

dialogue.speaker: Richard
dialogue.line: I'm going to turn around now, and when I do, I want you to know that my eyes will be closed.

dialogue.speaker: Richard
dialogue.line: I will keep them closed until you tell me you're completely inside and the door is shut.

dialogue.speaker: Richard
dialogue.line: Then I will open them, but only after counting to ten, so there's no possibility of any... awkwardness.

jokes.text: Scotch on the rocks with a twist? I remember.
jokes.setup: Monica about to order a drink
jokes.punchline: Richard remembers her exact drink preference from their past relationship
jokes.explanation: Standard 'he remembers everything' romantic beat. Shows their history but not particularly funny.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'll have a...

dialogue.speaker: Richard
dialogue.line: Cosmopolitan. You always order a Cosmopolitan.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: How did you remember that?

dialogue.speaker: Richard
dialogue.line: I remember everything about you, Monica.

jokes.text: No. That's art.
jokes.setup: Monica asking if he still smokes cigars
jokes.punchline: Richard pretentiously refers to his cigar as art
jokes.explanation: Perfect Richard pretension delivered matter-of-factly. Great economy and character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Do you still smoke cigars?

dialogue.speaker: Richard
dialogue.line: Well, I prefer to think of it as appreciating a fine art form. A cigar isn't merely tobacco—it's a carefully crafted expression of culture and refinement.

jokes.text: If it bothers you, I can put my art out.
jokes.setup: Just called his cigar 'art'
jokes.punchline: Continues the pretentious art metaphor for extinguishing a cigar
jokes.explanation: Good callback that extends the art metaphor. Shows Richard's commitment to his pretentious bit.

dialogue.speaker: Richard
dialogue.line: It's not just extinguishing it, it's a deliberate artistic statement. The way the smoke dissipates into the air... it's like watching a Rothko fade into obscurity.

jokes.text: Actually, I thought about you a couple months ago. Yeah, but it's because I had an eye exam, and I don't like my new eye doctor.
jokes.setup: Richard asking if Monica ever thinks about him romantically
jokes.punchline: Monica reveals she only thought of him because of medical disappointment
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection that starts romantic then becomes completely practical. Good deflection of his advances.

dialogue.speaker: Richard
dialogue.line: Do you ever think about me? Romantically?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, there was that one time...

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: But it was only because my gynecologist cancelled on me.

jokes.text: Edward Nevsky. Yeah, he's no good.
jokes.setup: Monica's eye doctor comparison
jokes.punchline: Richard immediately agrees about the eye doctor quality
jokes.explanation: Richard getting momentarily distracted by professional shop talk. Mild character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: My eye doctor is amazing. He's so good, he can tell what you're thinking just by looking in your eyes.

dialogue.speaker: Richard
dialogue.line: That's nothing. My eye doctor once told me I was going to die three weeks before it happened.

jokes.text: It's just good sense to back up your backup.
jokes.setup: Ross and Joey complaining about both being Phoebe's backup
jokes.punchline: Phoebe treats relationship insurance like computer data backup
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe logic applying technical concepts to relationships. Great economy and character voice.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You guys are like my relationship RAID array. Ross, you're the mirror drive. Joey, you're the backup drive.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Phoebe, that's not how relationships work.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh please. If Mike crashes, I need redundancy. You're both just sitting there waiting to be restored.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Did she just call me a backup drive?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: And before you ask, no, I don't have a tertiary. That's what Monica's for.

jokes.text: This kind of back talk isn't gonna fly when we're married.
jokes.setup: Ross and Joey arguing about the backup arrangement
jokes.punchline: Phoebe acts like they're already in a marriage relationship with her
jokes.explanation: Good Phoebe dominance moment that treats the backup plan like current reality.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Okay, okay, okay. So you guys are fighting about who gets to be with me if one of you dies? That's so sweet. But I have to tell you, I've already made my decision.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Who?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, I'm going with Chandler. He's better at spooning and he lets me pick the movie.

jokes.text: Ross is a good father. But Joey has a boat. This is hard.
jokes.setup: Phoebe having to choose between Ross and Joey as backup
jokes.punchline: Treats parenting ability and boat ownership as equivalent marriage qualifications
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe priorities that completely misunderstand what makes a good husband. Great character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I have to choose between Ross and Joey as my backup husband? Well, Ross is a paleontologist with a child, so he's clearly experienced with parenting. But Joey has a boat!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Those are basically the same thing when you're picking a husband.

jokes.text: Remember my promise: If we get married, three times a week.
jokes.setup: Phoebe trying to convince Ross and Joey not to switch to Rachel
jokes.punchline: Promises frequent sexual activity to sweeten the deal
jokes.explanation: Setup for the massage misdirection. Works because it sounds sexual but has innocent explanation.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You guys, you can't switch to Rachel! She's my friend!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Phoebe, we're just trying to save money on rent.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, I'll make it worth your while. I promise you guys frequent sexual activity.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: With each other!

jokes.text: I'm talking about massages. No, I'm not.
jokes.setup: Ross and Joey's shocked reaction to her 'three times a week' offer
jokes.punchline: Phoebe first clarifies it's massages, then immediately admits she meant sex
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection structure. Phoebe's honesty makes the reversal work perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: What? I give great massages!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Okay, fine, I meant sex.

jokes.text: Ross. Joey.
jokes.setup: Phoebe picking names from napkins
jokes.punchline: She clearly reads the opposite names from what she picked
jokes.explanation: Visual gag showing Phoebe manipulating the 'random' selection. Requires audience to catch the contradiction.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Okay, I'm picking a name... Brad Pitt.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God, it says 'Congratulations, you've won a lifetime supply of turtle wax.'

jokes.text: We should just switch. Absolutely.
jokes.setup: Both guys got matched with the person they didn't want
jokes.punchline: They immediately agree to switch, defeating the whole selection process
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the selection setup. Shows the whole process was meaningless from the start.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, this is awkward. I got matched with someone completely wrong for me.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, me too. This algorithm is terrible.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You know what? We should just switch.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, okay.

jokes.text: Maybe someone with a license to practice medicine. Or a mustache.
jokes.setup: Monica saying she needs to be with someone more mature
jokes.punchline: Richard describes himself in the most obvious terms possible
jokes.explanation: Richard's transparent self-promotion delivered with mock subtlety. The mustache detail is particularly good.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I need to be with someone more mature.

dialogue.speaker: Richard
dialogue.line: Well, I'm 21 years older than you.

jokes.text: Talking about pig sex over lunch, I don't want that!
jokes.setup: Monica contrasting Richard's romantic approach with Joey's earlier conversation
jokes.punchline: References Joey's pig mating philosophy from earlier
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows how Joey's 'wisdom' was actually hurting his relationship with Phoebe indirectly.

jokes.text: Nothing! Nothing! Nothing!
jokes.setup: Monica breaking down about how unfair her situation is
jokes.punchline: Escalating repetition showing her emotional breakdown
jokes.explanation: Emotional escalation moment that borders on dramatic. The repetition creates both comedy and pathos.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: This is so unfair! I can't believe this is happening to me!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: This is so unfair! This is so unfair!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: This is so unfair! This is so unfair! This is so unfair!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: THIS IS SO UNFAIR! THIS IS SO UNFAIR! THIS IS SO UNFAIR! THIS IS SO UNFAIR!

jokes.text: 10, 20 minutes, if you need it.
jokes.setup: Richard telling Monica to take as much time as she needs to think
jokes.punchline: Offers an absurdly short time frame for a life-changing decision
jokes.explanation: Perfect understatement that reveals Richard's impatience despite trying to seem understanding.

dialogue.speaker: Richard
dialogue.line: Take as much time as you need to think about this.

dialogue.speaker: Richard
dialogue.line: How does next Tuesday sound?

jokes.text: Well, all right, one thing happened.
jokes.setup: Chandler accusing Richard of telling Monica he loves her after Richard claims nothing happened
jokes.punchline: Richard's casual admission that he did profess his love
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery that makes a huge romantic gesture sound trivial.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You told her you loved her?

dialogue.speaker: Richard
dialogue.line: Well, yeah.

jokes.text: You made my girlfriend think!
jokes.setup: Chandler angry about Richard's interference
jokes.punchline: Treats Monica's thinking as a negative outcome
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler line that reveals his insecurity about Monica reconsidering their relationship.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I can't believe Richard keeps interfering! Every time Monica tries to make a decision, he's there telling her what to do.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You know what? At this point, I'm just grateful when Monica actually thinks for herself. That's like a positive outcome at this point.

jokes.text: It was working till you showed up, you big tree.
jokes.setup: Chandler explaining his plan was working before Richard interfered
jokes.punchline: Insults Richard by comparing him to a tree (highlighting his height/imposing presence)
jokes.explanation: 'Big tree' is a perfectly Chandler-esque insult that's both childish and oddly specific.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: My plan was working fine until Richard came along. The guy's so tall and imposing, he makes a sequoia look like a bonsai!

jokes.text: You know, Richard, you are a good guy. I know. I hate that.
jokes.setup: Chandler acknowledging Richard's decency after Richard encourages him to pursue Monica
jokes.punchline: Chandler's honest admission that he hates Richard's goodness
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Chandler's conflicted feelings about his 'rival' being genuinely decent.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You know what? You're a good man. A really good man. And I hate you for it.

jokes.text: There's a reason why girls don't do this!
jokes.setup: Monica struggling to get down on one knee to propose
jokes.punchline: Realizes the practical/physical challenges of the traditional male proposal position
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about gender role practicalities. Monica's typical overthinking approach.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Okay, I'm going down on one knee. Here we go...

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh God, my back. Why do men do this? This is awful!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: And how am I supposed to get back up? Do I just... roll?

jokes.text: I knew you were likely to take a wife!
jokes.setup: Monica and Chandler just got engaged
jokes.punchline: Joey references his own earlier formal language about Chandler being 'unlikely to take a wife'
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that shows Joey maintaining his formal speech pattern while reversing his earlier assessment.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I just want to say, I always thought Chandler was unlikely to take a wife, and I was wrong.

jokes.text: This is the least jealous I've ever been!
jokes.setup: Monica and Chandler's engagement celebration
jokes.punchline: Rachel continues the jealousy percentage theme but now claims zero jealousy
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Rachel's arc from jealous to genuinely happy. Nice completion of the running gag.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Zero percent jealous.

jokes.text: Oh, no, wait! This is wrong! Ross isn't here!
jokes.setup: Everyone celebrating Monica and Chandler's engagement
jokes.punchline: Rachel suddenly realizes they're celebrating without one of the core friends
jokes.explanation: Rachel being considerate about including Ross in the celebration. More sweet than funny.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. Where's Joey?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: He's on a date.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What? He's missing our engagement party?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: It's not like he knew about it.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Still! This is huge! We're all supposed to be here together!

jokes.text: He's done it three times. He knows what it's about!
jokes.setup: Rachel worried about Ross missing the engagement celebration
jokes.punchline: Chandler dismissively references Ross's multiple divorces
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler snark that references Ross's relationship history. Great way to end the episode with his trademark sarcasm.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Where is Ross? He's missing the engagement celebration!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh, he's probably at home practicing his vows. You know, so he can get them right this time. And the time after that. And the time after that.

insight: Proposal episode underdelivers at 59 — emotional weight outpaces comedy throughout.
